vitals.terrain: Hilly with climbs to Western Ghats

course_description.overview: Single-day road cycling sportive from Pune across the Deccan Plateau, climbing over the Sahyadri mountains (Western Ghats) to Mahabaleshwar, featuring steep ascents and descents through forested cliffs.

key_segments.name: Pune to Western Ghats climb
key_segments.description: Initial flat to rolling terrain on Deccan Plateau leading to major ascent over Sahyadri mountains.

key_segments.name: Sahyadri cliffs to Mahabaleshwar
key_segments.description: Steep climbs and drops through dense forests to hill station finish.

course_description.difficulty_narrative: Challenging due to significant elevation gain over Western Ghats with cliffhanger descents; marketed as India's most popular single-day cycling sportive.

logistics.getting_there: Start in Pune, accessible by air (Pune Airport), train, or road; finish in Mahabaleshwar, reachable by bus or taxi from Pune.
logistics.accommodation: Hotels and guesthouses abundant in Pune and Mahabaleshwar; book early for event weekend.
logistics.registration_details: Organized by Inspire India; check ultracycling.com or inspireindia.net.in for entries, typically opens months in advance.
logistics.bike_logistics: Fly or transport bike to Pune; road event, standard road bikes suitable.
logistics.local_considerations: November weather mild (20-30°C); watch for traffic on public roads, carry hydration.

final_verdict.one_liner: Iconic Indian sportive with brutal Ghats climbs and stunning plateau-to-hill views.
final_verdict.who_is_it_for: Road cyclists seeking a tough one-day challenge in India's premier sportive scene.
final_verdict.is_it_worth_it: Yes, for the scenery, community, and status as Maharashtra's top cycling event.
pro_tips: Train for sustained climbs (Tambini Ghat key)
Start early to beat Pune traffic and heat
Hydrate heavily; carry spares for mixed road conditions
Stay in Mahabaleshwar post-race for recovery.
